title: Vue Filters Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of Vue 2 filters and the Vue 3 transition to computed properties/methods.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary role of a 'Filter' in older versions of Vue.js (Vue 2)?
options: To apply common text formatting to an expression in a template.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the character used to combine multiple filters in a template (the pipe syntax)?
Is the 'filters' feature still supported in Vue 3?
options: Yes, it is still the recommended way to format text.
No, it was officially removed in Vue 3.

questions.question: What is the recommended alternative for filters in Vue 3 and modern development?
options: Use computed properties or standard JavaScript methods.
Only write formatting logic in HTML comments.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the benefit of using computed properties instead of filters for data formatting?
options: Better performance and easier to update/maintain since the logic is in JavaScript.
They make the HTML file smaller.
They only work with local data.
